rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 3, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication is granted in part, and denied in part. Motion is denied as to No. 1, that Electric service to Ferry Building constitutes a "new account" under the Master Settlement Agreement. Motion is granted as to the remaining issues: 2) There has been a material change in the use and activity at the Ferry Building following the remodeling project; 3) Electric service to the Ferry Building Investors at the Ferry Building does not qualify as municipal load under the 1987 Interconnection Agreeemnt. 4)PG&E is not required by the 1987 Interconnection Agreement to transport Hetch Hetchy power to Ferry Building Investors or subtenants at the Ferry Building; 5) CCSF is prohibited by the 1987 Interconnection Agreement from selling PG&E transmitted electricity to Ferry Building Investors, or subtenants at the Ferry Building; and, 6) PG&E is entitled to serve electricity to Ferry Building Investors and its subtenants at the Ferry Building. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: TERESA SPALDING VS. MAXIM BUNGALOWS COFRESI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Ruling, Denying Mtn For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 3, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT IMPACT NET WORTH LLC, JAMES CATLEDGE, IMPACT REAL ESTATE, LLC, NETWORTH SOLUTIONS, LLC, and, IMPACT, INC. Motion For Relief From Ruling, Denying Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Action. Motion for relief from ruling is denied. There is no basis for relief under CCP section 473(b). Legal problem posed not complex and debatable, no excusable neglect shown. (See State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. vs. Pietak (2001) 90 Cal.App. 4th 600.) This is an improper motion for reconsideration. (See Wiz Technology, Inc. vs. Coopers & Lybrand (2003) 106 Cal.App. 4th 1, 17.) Request for sanctions is denied without prejudice. =(302/CWW/JW)
